---
title: "LLM + Wikipedia Shiny App"
format: html
editor: visual
server: shiny
---

```{r}
install.packages(
  "duckdb",
  repos = c("https://duckdb.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org")
)
install.packages("WikipediR")
install.packages("shiny", "openai")
install.packages("dplyr")
```




```{r}
library(shiny)
library(WikipediR)
library(openai)
library(dplyr)

key_path <- "openai_key.txt"
openai_key <- readLines(key_path, warn = FALSE)
Sys.setenv(OPENAI_API_KEY = openai_key)
# ----rewrite query----
rewrite_query <- function(question) {
  response <- create_chat_completion(
    model = "gpt-4.1-mini", # really any cheap model ehre is prob ok
    messages = list(
      list(
        role = "system",
        content = "Rewrite the user's question as a short keyword search query for Wikipedia. Return only the search query."
      ),
      list(role = "user", content = question)
    )
  )

  query <- response$choices[1, "message.content"]

  # sanitize
  query <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9 \\-]", "", query)
  trimws(query)
}

# ----extract wikitext ----
extract_wikitext <- function(wt) {
  pages <- wt$query$pages
  page <- pages[[1]] # safe because there's only one page

  if (
    !is.null(page$revisions) &&
      length(page$revisions) > 0 &&
      !is.null(page$revisions[[1]]$slots$main$`*`)
  ) {
    return(page$revisions[[1]]$slots$main$`*`)
  }

  return("")
}

# ---- Wikipedia Search Function ----
fetch_wikipedia <- function(query, limit = 5) {
  # 1. Search
  url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php"
  res <- httr::GET(
    url,
    query = list(
      action = "query",
      list = "search",
      srsearch = query,
      format = "json",
      srlimit = limit
    )
  )

  json <- httr::content(res, "parsed")
  titles <- json$query$search$title

  # 2. Fetch wikitext for each title
  pages <- lapply(titles, function(title) {
    wt <- wikipediR::wikitext_query(
      titles = title,
      properties = "revisions",
      rvprop = "content",
      rvslots = "main",
      language = "en",
      project = "wikipedia"
    )

    text_raw <- wt$query$pages[[1]]$revisions[[1]]$slots$main$`*`

    list(
      title = title,
      url = paste0("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/", gsub(" ", "_", title)),
      text = text_raw
    )
  })

  message("== Wikitext lengths ==")
  print(sapply(pages, function(p) nchar(p$text)))

  return(pages)
}


# ---- LLM Summarizer ----
summarize_with_llm <- function(question, pages) {
  retrieved_text <- paste(
    sapply(pages, function(p) {
      paste0("### ", p$title, "\n", substr(p$text, 1, 1200), "...")
    }),
    collapse = "\n\n"
  )

  prompt <- paste0(
    "You are assisting a user by answering their question using ONLY the Wikipedia excerpts provided.

",
    "User question: ",
    question,
    "

",
    "Wikipedia excerpts:
",
    retrieved_text,
    "

",
    "Using ALL of the provided Wikipedia excerpts, write a single unified answer.
    Synthesize the information across all pages. Do NOT list the excerpts separately.

    At the end, include a Citations section based ONLY on the pages provided.  
    For each page you used, output one bullet in the following format (There should be 3-5):

    - TITLE — URL

    Where:
    - TITLE is the actual Wikipedia page title (e.g., Space Race)
    - URL is the actual URL provided for that page (e.g., https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Race)

    Do NOT output placeholders like 'Page Title' or 'URL'."
  )

  response <- create_chat_completion(
    model = "gpt-4.1",
    messages = list(
      list(
        role = "system",
        content = "You summarize Wikipedia results accurately."
      ),
      list(role = "user", content = prompt)
    )
  )
  print("=== DEBUG: prompt sent to model ===")
  print(prompt)
  answer <- response$choices[1, "message.content"]
  return(answer)
}

# ---- Extract Citations -----
extract_citations <- function(answer_text) {
  lines <- unlist(strsplit(answer_text, "\n"))

  # lines starting with "- " and containing " — "
  cite_lines <- grep("^\\- ", lines, value = TRUE)

  citations <- lapply(cite_lines, function(line) {
    parts <- unlist(strsplit(sub("^\\- ", "", line), " — "))
    list(
      title = parts[1],
      url = parts[2]
    )
  })

  return(citations)
}

# ---- clean response ----
clean_answer <- function(answer_text) {
  lines <- unlist(strsplit(answer_text, "\n"))

  # keep everything until we hit the line starting with "Citations:"
  idx <- grep("^Citations:", lines)

  if (length(idx) > 0) {
    cleaned <- lines[1:(idx[1] - 1)]
  } else {
    cleaned <- lines
  }

  paste(cleaned, collapse = "\n")
}

# ---- UI ----
ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$style(HTML(
    "
  .answer-box pre {
    white-space: pre-wrap !important;
    word-break: break-word !important;
  }
"
  )),

  titlePanel("Ask Wikipedia (Powered by LLM)"),

  textInput("question", "Enter a question:", width = "60%"),
  actionButton("ask", "Ask"),
  hr(),

  h3("Answer"),
  div(
    class = "answer-box",
    verbatimTextOutput("answer")
  ),

  h3("Citations"),
  uiOutput("citations")
)

# ---- Server ----
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  results <- eventReactive(input$ask, {
    req(input$question)
    pages <- fetch_wikipedia(rewrite_query(input$question))
    answer_raw <- summarize_with_llm(input$question, pages)
    print("=== DEBUG: page titles ===")
    print(sapply(pages, function(p) p$title))
    print("=== DEBUG: raw LLM output ===")
    print(answer_raw)
    answer_clean <- clean_answer(answer_raw)
    citations <- extract_citations(answer_raw)
    list(
      answer = answer_raw,
      citations = citations
    )
  })

  output$answer <- renderText({
    req(results())
    results()$answer
  })

  output$citations <- renderUI({
    req(results())
    lapply(results()$citations, function(c) {
      tags$div(
        tags$a(href = c$url, c$title, target = "_blank")
      )
    })
  })
}


shinyApp(ui, server)
```
